title: narcissus rugilobus
intro: Narcissus rugilobus is a rare and little-known species of bulbous perennial plant, native to Western Australia, belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Australian Aboriginal medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Narcissus rugilobus include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, the bulbs are used in herbal remedies, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
